title: Vignea stricta
intro:
Unfortunately, I can not verify the plant Vignea stricta, but I can describe a similar plant named Vigna stricta.

Vigna stricta, also known as Indian snail bean or wild rice bean, is a perennial legume native to tropical Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Vigna stricta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vigna stricta include decoctions, infusions, and powders, often used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation, as well as to promote overall well-being.
